definition:
1. Linos , a mythical minstrel, son of Apollo and Urania (Calliope), teacher of Orpheus, (Theocritus Poeta Bucolicus), etc. 
2. as appellative, the song  or lay of Linos , sung by a boy to the cithara while the vintagers are at work, Λίνον ὑπὸ καλὸν ἄειδεν sang the  lovely lay of Linos  in accompaniment, (Iliad by Homer):—;compare αἴλινος which is a mournful song.  (ML)
